title: Water Department Public Meeting for Westminster Avenue Area Neighbors
summary: The City of Syracuse Department of Water will host a public meeting on June 26, 2024, to inform Westminster Avenue area residents about the Westminster Pump Station project, which includes replacement of water mains and lead-containing service lines at no cost to property owners. The project affects approximately 50 homes in the Westcott neighborhood and is part of the Water Department's long-term infrastructure plan to improve water quality and maintain safe pressure.

political_signal.description: Public notice demonstrates administration commitment to infrastructure modernization and lead remediation through transparent community engagement process.

processing_notes: Author listed as 'Unknown' in source metadata
Mechanical extraction flagged 'Syracuse Fire Department' but article references fire protection as secondary benefit; Water Department is primary subject
No dollar amounts disclosed in press release
Meeting date (June 26) is 8 days after publication date (June 18)
Geographic scope limited to Westcott neighborhood area (Westminster Avenue, Maryland Avenue, Kensington Road)
